slots: Forest lodge check-in, safari briefing, zone allocation
Reach Chandrapur or Moharli gate area by midday. Collect permits, meet your naturalist, and understand core vs buffer zone rules before your first drive.
The afternoon slot often rewards patience — tigers move toward water as heat builds. Scan bamboo edges and Irai reservoir margins for sambar, gaur, and sloth bear.
Early night — tomorrow’s morning safari starts before dawn. Many lodges offer short nature walks or documentary screenings on Tadoba’s conservation story.
Tiger tracking, bird chorus, misty meadows
The classic Tadoba experience: cold dawn air, alarm calls from langurs, and the chance of a striped silhouette crossing the track. Carry binoculars for raptors and woodland birds.
Skip a second drive if you prefer recovery — or book a buffer-zone route for landscape photography and wetland species away from the busiest gates.
Evening light softens the teak canopy. Experienced drivers read pugmarks and alarm calls — stay quiet and let the forest reveal itself.
Use your remaining slot for a zone you have not covered, or revisit a productive meadow. Check out by late morning for onward travel to Nagpur airport or Pench.
Many visitors route through Nagpur — tarri poha breakfast, Sitabuldi fort views, and saoji curry before flying out.
Plan departures around safari end times; monsoon and summer slots fill weeks ahead — book return transport accordingly.
